OPPOSITION TO RESPONDENTS OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM GOVERNMENT CLAIMS FILING REQUIREMENTS FILED BY PETITIONER BATISTE SR, DAVID D SHEPPARD, ROSE M

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS DAVID BATISTE, AND, ROSE SHEPPARD MOTION OF PETITION FOR COURT TO ACCEPT PETITIONERS FILING OF CLAIM #09-02772 FOR NOT BEING A DUPLICATE FILING, AND FOR COURT ORDER RELIEVING PETITIONER FROM THE PROVISIONS OF GOVERNMENT CODE 945.4. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION IS DENIED. CLAIM IS TIME BARRED. CLAIM RELATING TO DAMAGE TO REAL PROPERTY MUST BE SUBMITTED NO LATER THAN ONE YEAR AFTER ACCRUAL. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 911.2(A). DAMAGES OCCURRED IN 2002 AND 2005. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. NO EXPRESS OR IMPLIED ASSURANCE THAT DEFENDANT WOULD COVER PLAINTIFFS' DAMAGE MADE SUCH THAT DEFENDANT ESTOPPED FROM BRINGING A STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Court To Accept Petitioners Filing Of Claim #09-02772 For Not Being A Duplicate Filing. Court Order Relieving Petitioner From The Provisions Of Goverment Code 945.4
Off Calendar Jul-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS DAVID BATISTE, and, ROSE SHEPPARD Motion Of Petition for Court to Accept Petitioners Filing of Claim #09-02772 for Not Being a Duplicate Filing, and for Court Order Relieving Petitioner from the Provisions of Government Code 945.4. Appearances: David Batiste, Petitioner in Pro Per and moving party; Daniel A. Zaheer, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3822, appearing for Respondent CCSF, and opposing party. The Court allows argument. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition is denied. Claim is time barred. Claim relating to damage to real property must be submitted no later than one year after accrual. Government Code section 911.2(a). Damages occurred in 2002 and 2005. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. No express or implied assurance that defendant would cover plaintiffs' damage made such that defendant estopped from bringing a statute of limitations defense. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
